_doc: Generic AI Agent driver — reasons over a task using an LLM node and executes actions on topology nodes as tools.
label: Generic AI Agent
description: AI agent driver — given a task, reasons step-by-step using an LLM node and executes actions on topology nodes exposed as tools.

llm_node.label: LLM Node
llm_node.description: LLM node this agent uses for reasoning

max_steps.label: Max Steps
max_steps.description: Maximum reasoning iterations before the agent stops

max_tool_calls.label: Max Tool Calls
max_tool_calls.description: Hard cap on total tool executions per task (0 = unlimited)

context.label: Context
context.description: Domain knowledge injected into every task — describe the environment, criticality, topology purpose, etc.
context.placeholder: This is a lab environment with 3 nodes. Node r1 acts as the default gateway.

rules.label: Rules
rules.description: Behavioral constraints in natural language — what the agent must or must not do
rules.placeholder:
Never restart a node without logging the reason.
Only perform read-only actions unless the task explicitly requests a change.

allowed_tools.label: Allowed Tools
allowed_tools.description: Tools this agent is allowed to call. Empty = all discovered tools are allowed.

dry_run.label: Dry Run
dry_run.description: When enabled, tools are described but NOT executed — safe for testing tasks

system_prompt.label: System Prompt (override)
system_prompt.description: Fully replace the default agent system prompt (context and rules are still appended)

agent-run.label: Run Task
agent-run.command: # Agentic loop: LLM + tool_use over topology nodes — task via request body {"task": "..."}

agent-tools.label: List Tools
agent-tools.command: # Discover all ai-tool nodes in topology and list their exposed actions
